fields.icd_description: Unspecified tear of medial meniscus, right knee
fields.icd_conflict_detail: The referral form (document 1) lists ICD-10 code M23.201 (Derangement of medial meniscus, right knee), while both the clinical notes (document 2) and prescription (document 3) consistently use S83.005A (Unspecified tear of medial meniscus, right knee). Per resolution protocol, the clinical notes and prescription take authority over the referral form; S83.005A is selected as the correct code. Additionally, S83.005A is more clinically specific given the post-surgical rehabilitation context noted in the clinical notes.

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription both consistently use S83.005A and carry greater clinical authority than the referral form; the post-surgical rehabilitation context further supports the acute tear code S83.005A over the chronic derangement code M23.201.

fields.priority_reason: The case involves a post-surgical rehabilitation protocol following an acute meniscal tear, an ICD code conflict requiring resolution, and the clinical notes indicate the patient is unable to safely ambulate without the assistive device, creating immediate fall risk.
fields.notes: 1) ICD-10 conflict detected: referral form uses M23.201 but clinical notes and prescription use S83.005A — resolved in favor of S83.005A. Recommend confirming correct code with Dr. Sarah Chen's office before finalizing. 2) Authorization Ref # is missing — authorization from adjuster Linda Torres at Pacific Mutual Workers Comp should be obtained prior to dispensing. 3) Patient height/weight documented as 6'3" / 252 lbs — ensure L1820 knee brace ordered is rated for appropriate weight capacity and correct sizing is confirmed. 4) Clinical notes describe a post-surgical rehabilitation protocol, which may affect brace specifications (standard vs. post-op brace); verify with prescribing physician whether L1820 (standard knee orthosis) is the appropriate HCPCS code vs. a post-surgical alternative. 5) Preferred appointment window was not provided — outreach to patient or adjuster recommended to schedule delivery.

fired_rules.source: CMS DME MAC Jurisdiction D — Prior Authorization Requirements
fired_rules.message: Workers compensation claim — authorization reference number required for E0143 billing. Missing auth ref blocks claim submission.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

email_body:
Dear Linda Torres,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding a pending durable medical equipment (DME) referral for your insured, **Kathleen Sanchez**, under claim number **WC-2026-084474**.

In reviewing the referral, we have identified the following missing information that is required to move forward with processing:

- **Authorization Reference Number (auth_ref)** *(Hard Block — routing is on hold pending receipt)*
- **Appointment Window (appt_window)** *(Hard Block — routing is on hold pending receipt)*
- **Preferred Language (language)*

Please note that the first two items above are hard blocks, meaning the referral cannot be routed or scheduled until they are received. We kindly ask that you provide all outstanding information **within 24 business hours** of this notice to avoid any further delays in care for Ms. Sanchez.

You may reply directly to this email or contact our intake team at your earliest convenience. We appreciate your prompt attention to this matter and look forward to your response.

Sincerely,

**Coastal DME Intake Agent**
Coastal DME

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription both consistently use S83.005A and carry greater clinical authority than the referral form; the post-surgical rehabilitation context further supports the acute tear code S83.005A over the chronic derangement code M23.201.
